name: menu-button
description: A button that opens an anchored action menu of one-shot commands

meta.name: MenuButton
meta.description: A button that opens an anchored action menu of one-shot commands

props.name: items
props.description: Action items: { id, label, onSelect?, disabled? }

props.name: align
props.description: Aligns the menu to the start or end edge of the trigger

examples.title: Basic action menu
examples.description: A secondary button that opens a list of actions
examples.code:
<MenuButton
  label="Actions"
  items={[
    { id: 'edit', label: 'Edit', onSelect: () => edit() },
    { id: 'duplicate', label: 'Duplicate', onSelect: () => duplicate() },
    { id: 'delete', label: 'Delete', onSelect: () => remove(), disabled: !canDelete },
  ]}
/>

examples.title: End-aligned, primary
examples.description: Aligns the menu to the trigger end edge
examples.code:
<MenuButton
  label="Create"
  variant="primary"
  align="end"
  items={[
    { id: 'doc', label: 'New document', onSelect: createDoc },
    { id: 'folder', label: 'New folder', onSelect: createFolder },
  ]}
/>

whenToUse: Exposing a small set of one-shot actions behind a single labeled button
Toolbar or header overflow actions where each item runs a command on activation
Action lists that need ArrowUp/ArrowDown roving focus and Enter/Space activation
whenNotToUse: Choosing and persisting a value from options — use Select
Right-click contextual actions on a target element — use ContextMenu
A single action with no list — use a plain Button
Rich interactive content (forms, pickers) rather than a list of commands — use Popover

antiPatterns.bad: Rendering checkbox or radio state inside MenuButton items and treating selection as a chosen value
antiPatterns.good: Use Select or MultiSelect for value selection; MenuButton items are one-shot actions that close the menu on activation
antiPatterns.why: role="menuitem" items do not model a selected value and dismiss the menu when activated, so they cannot represent persistent choices

antiPatterns.bad: Leaving the trigger label empty with no visible text and no labels.open override
antiPatterns.good: Provide visible label text, or pass labels.open / rely on the built-in i18n accessible name
antiPatterns.why: A trigger with no accessible name is unusable for screen-reader and voice-control users

related.name: Menu
related.reason: Use Menu for fully composable trigger + item children; MenuButton is the prop-driven convenience form

related.name: ContextMenu
related.reason: Use ContextMenu when actions are triggered by right-click on a target rather than a button

related.name: Select
related.reason: Use Select when the user is picking a value instead of firing an action

related.name: Button
related.reason: The trigger is a styled button that opens the menu

intent.a11yRationale: The trigger is a real <button> exposing aria-haspopup="menu", aria-expanded reflecting open state, and aria-controls pointing at the role="menu" panel. ArrowDown/Enter/Space open the menu and move focus to the first enabled item, ArrowUp opens and focuses the last item, ArrowUp/ArrowDown rove focus between items with wrap-around, disabled items are aria-disabled and skipped by roving focus, Escape closes and restores focus to the trigger, and Tab closes without trapping focus. DismissableLayer provides outside-pointer and Escape dismissal, and high-contrast plus reduced-motion preferences are honored in CSS.

flexibility.note: Trigger and menu styling must resolve to the listed --cascivo-* tokens
Each item label accepts arbitrary ReactNode; onSelect defines the action
variant and size choose among the standard button looks and dimensions
